current.summary: Capability monitor pipeline: brainstorm + 5-round panel review complete (5/5 consensus), design doc updated with 40 panel changes, ready for plan writing in next session

activeWork.description: Building a deterministic capability monitor pipeline (syllago capmon subcommand) to replace expensive LLM-based provider capability audits. Design is complete and panel-reviewed; next step is writing the implementation plan with subagent-sized task chunks.
activeWork.progress: COMPLETED IN THIS SESSION: (1) Brainstorm phase with ~9 design questions answered (syllago capmon subcommand, twice-daily GH Actions cron, YAML data layer with per-field refs, fully deterministic extraction via gotreesitter + goquery + goldmark + go/parser, chromedp for Cursor only — empirically verified in 4 sec). (2) 5-round panel review with 5 panelists (remy, engineer, operator, maintainer, security) via JSONL message bus at .develop/capmon-panel-bus.jsonl — 26 messages across rounds 0-5. (3) Consensus reached: 5-of-5 proceed (1 agree from engineer, 4 agree_with_reservations from remy/operator/maintainer/security), zero dissents. (4) Design doc updated with all 35 mandatory + 5 nice-to-have panel changes — grew from 535 to ~910 lines. Panel Review Record section added. NEXT: dispatch plan-writing subagent against the updated design to produce subagent-sized task chunks (per user direction: 'we can break this up as needed'). Subsequent phases: quality review, validation, beads creation with Phase B blocker analysis, execution.

recentDecisions: chromedp for Cursor docs is the only option — llms.txt genuinely does not exist, empirically verified via WebFetch + direct test (4 sec fetch, 34KB content, no bot challenge). All other providers use direct HTTP (llms-full.txt for Windsurf, GitHub raw for VS Code, etc.)

gotreesitter (pure Go tree-sitter reimplementation) is the parser for TypeScript + Rust — zero CGO. microsoft/typescript-go is the intended long-term target once its API stabilizes. Extractor interface decouples dispatch from specific parser library.
Per-provider YAML is source of truth; per-content-type YAML is generated. References table with per-field `refs:` lists gives full provenance without verbosity bloat.

Two-job GH Actions workflow in single file: fetch-extract with permissions: {}, report with write permissions, SHA-256 artifact verification across the seam. SHA-pinned actions via Dependabot, Docker images via Renovate datasource.
Single source of truth: hooks spec tables (events.md §4, blocking-matrix.md §2, capabilities.md, tools.md §1) will be regenerated from provider-capabilities/*.yaml via syllago capmon generate
Panel review uses JSONL message bus with serialized round-5 dispatches to avoid race conditions (clobbered security round 3 had to be manually restored from agent task output)
User's preference: break execution into focused subagent-sized task chunks, not one massive execution agent
nextSteps: Dispatch plan-writing subagent against the panel-reviewed design at docs/plans/2026-04-08-capability-monitor-pipeline-design.md — instruct it to produce subagent-sized task chunks organized into ~8-10 phases
Run quality review subagent against the plan (haiku, 10-point checklist including per-task granularity)
Run validation subagent for design-to-plan parity
Create beads (1 epic + ~40-50 task beads with dependencies wired)
Run Phase B blocker analysis before execution
Execute — dispatch focused subagents per-task or per-phase cluster
Clean up two stray 0-byte files in repo root: ', expected_contains: ' and ', min_results: <int | omit>, updated_at:' — artifacts from a subagent heredoc mishap during design update. Safe to delete.

context.notes:
PANEL REVIEW ARTIFACTS: Full bus at .develop/capmon-panel-bus.jsonl (26 messages, rounds 0-5). Verbatim backup at .develop/capmon-panel-bus.r5-complete.jsonl. Per-round backups: .develop/capmon-panel-bus.r{1,2,3,4,5}-complete.jsonl. Panel verdict tally: engineer agree, remy/operator/maintainer/security agree_with_reservations — 5-of-5 consensus, 0 dissents.

PANEL CONVERGED ON 9 CLUSTERS: A=two-job workflow + chromedp, B=run manifest, C=structured selector schema, D=parser-agnostic Extractor interface, E=seed idempotency, F=PR dedup + body template, G=exit classes + staleness sentinel, H=security controls (sanitization + SSRF allowlist + PR body escaping), I=schema version evolution policy.

KEY SECURITY CONSTRAINTS FROM PANEL (all must land together in one PR): (1) Two-job split with permissions: {} on fetch-extract, (2) chromedp services block with cap-drop=ALL --no-new-privileges --read-only, (3) sanitizeExtractedString with YAML structural char percent-encoding, (4) validateSourceURL with https-only + reserved IP blocking (127.0.0.0/8, 169.254.0.0/16, 100.64.0.0/10, ::1, fe80::/10), (5) PR body template writes extracted content directly to io.Writer (never into text/template data map), (6) yaml.v3 with explicit Tag: !!str on all extracted string fields (prevents type coercion on 'true' / 'null' event names), (7) sanitizeSlug applied to both PR body AND branch name construction.

KEY OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS FROM PANEL: (1) if: always() at step level on upload-artifact (NOT job level — panics bypass job-level), (2) continue-on-error: false on report job (peter-evans silently succeeds on clean tree), (3) staleness sentinel as third job on offset cron detects missed runs, (4) six exit classes 0-5 including class 5 for .capmon-pause sentinel.

RESERVATIONS RECORDED: (a) gotreesitter dependency review needs a named owner in Bootstrap Sequence step 1a — has concrete 5-item checklist, (b) Docker SHA pinning via Renovate docker datasource (not Dependabot which only covers Actions), (c) microsoft/typescript-go named as long-term migration target.

EXISTING UNCOMMITTED WORK (48 files): primarily the panel bus + design doc updates + state.json for THIS feature. No commits pending from hooks spec work — that was all pushed earlier in session. Need to commit the capmon design + panel bus + handoff in the next session (or beginning of this one if user wants to commit before clearing).

STRAY FILES TO DELETE: Two 0-byte files in repo root with whitespace-containing names — ', expected_contains: ' and ', min_results: <int | omit>, updated_at:' — created by the design-update subagent's heredoc. Harmless but should be deleted. Security hook blocks bulk rm -rf so deletion must be narrow.

SESSION CONTEXT: This is session 3+ of a long day that shipped hooks spec modularization (committed: 7d55ee4), hooks capability audit (committed: 1696df8), provider-formats tracking decision (committed: 7c7130d), then started capmon pipeline brainstorm which led here.

USER DIRECTION FOR EXECUTION: Break work into focused subagent-sized task chunks, not one massive execution agent. Per user: 'we can break this up as needed.' Plan writing subagent should organize tasks into ~8-10 phases with ~3-6 tasks per phase, each task scoped so a single focused subagent can complete it without juggling too many concerns. Parallel execution where phases are independent.
